title: alloy-cli source pin manifest v1
description: Per-source-kind URL + SHA256 pin file consumed by alloy-cli's toolchain installer.  Lives under data/sources/<source>.json and ships inside the alloy-cli wheel.  This is the trust boundary: the runtime never opens a URL not declared here.

schema_version.description: SemVer of the manifest schema this file targets.

source.description: Source kind.  Vendor-source tools are NEVER pinned here — their distribution is EULA-gated.

_pending_verification.description: When true, sha256 fields in this file are placeholders (zero-padded) awaiting a refresh_source_pins.py run.  Removing the flag is the contract for declaring a pin file production-ready.

_notes.description: Optional contributor-facing free-text note (e.g. how the URLs were sourced).

host_triple.description: Composite os-arch identifier.  Closed enum; new arches require a schema bump.

tool.description: Canonical tool name; must match the family manifest's `tool` field.

version.description: Exact SemVer-ish pin (no ranges).  Accepts both `-` and `_` as suffix separators so vendor-specific stamps like Espressif's `14.2.0_20240906` round-trip.

udev_rules.description: Linux udev rules text shipped with this tool's release.  Required when the family manifest declares `udev_required: true`; ignored otherwise.

hosts.description: Per-host download definition.  At least one host triple must be present; missing hosts surface as `family-toolchain-installer-unsupported-host` at install time.

unsupported_hosts.description: Host triples upstream does not publish a release for (documented absence so contributors can tell 'missing pin' from 'no upstream binary').

sha256.description: Lower-case hex SHA-256 of the artefact at `url`.  Placeholder zero-padded value is allowed only when the parent file declares `_pending_verification: true`.

archive_kind.description: How to unpack the artefact.  `bin` means single-file binary (e.g. probe-rs ships a static binary on some hosts) — copy-as-is into the store.

extract_to_subdir.description: Optional: if the archive contains a single top-level directory (e.g. xpack tarballs), name it here so the toolchain manager can flatten the extraction.  Empty / missing means 'extract at the store root'.

binaries.description: Relative paths (within the extracted artefact, after `extract_to_subdir` flattening) of every binary callers will resolve.  The first entry is the canonical primary binary.

size_bytes.description: Optional artefact size; used by `alloy toolchain install --dry-run` to print the planned download total without HEADing every URL.
